title: Payera bakeriana
taxonomy: Payera bakeriana is classified as a species within the Apocynaceae family, which includes plants commonly known as dogbanes or oleander dock. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships to other species within the Apocynaceae family. The genus Payera is part of the subfamily Rauvoloideae, which consists of plants that are mostly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Payera bakeriana is a relatively rare species, and its classification is closely related to other species in the Payera genus. Further research is necessary to fully understand the classification and relationships of Payera bakeriana within the Apocynaceae family.
uses: Payera bakeriana, a plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The leaves and stems of Payera bakeriana are believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
